elements.text:
Figure 27.3-7. Main wind force resisting system: net pressure coefficient, CN, for open buildings with free roofs (θ ≤ 45°, γ = 90°, 270°).

This figure provides net pressure coefficients (CN) for main wind force resisting systems in open buildings with free roofs at low angles (θ ≤ 45°) when wind direction is perpendicular to the ridge (γ = 90°, 270°). It shows three roof configurations (monoslope, pitched, trough) with corresponding pressure coefficient values based on horizontal distance from windward edge and roof angle.

[Structured: {"type":"diagram","nodes":[{"label":"Monoslope Roof","type":"diagram","details":["Shows roof with length L, height h, angle θ, wind direction γ = 90°"]},{"label":"Pitched Roof","type":"diagram","details":["Shows pitched roof with length L, height h, angle θ, wind direction γ = 90°"]},{"label":"Trough Roof","type":"diagram","details":["Shows trough configuration with length L, height h, angle θ, wind direction γ = 90°"]}],"edges":[]}]
elements.caption: Figure 27.3-7. Main wind force resisting system: net pressure coefficient, CN, for open buildings with free roofs (θ ≤ 45°, γ = 90°, 270°).

elements.text: **Notes** 1. CN denotes net pressures (contributions from top and bottom surfaces). 2. Clear wind flow denotes relatively unobstructed wind flow with blockage less than or equal to 50%. Obstructed wind flow denotes objects below roof inhibiting wind flow (>50% blockage). 3. Plus and minus signs signify pressures acting toward and away from the top roof surface, respectively. 4. All load cases shown for each roof angle shall be investigated.
